acceptanceSignals: Vendor docs and operations filters are functional.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Vendor Portal MVP in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No contract-drift blocker found for this feature in the current workspace. Backend interface changes for Vendor Portal MVP are synchronized across handlers, OpenAPI artifacts (`contract/openapi/openapi.json` and `contract/openapi/openapi.yaml`), and regenerated TS client artifacts (`contract/generated/ts-client/...`) for the affected vendor operations (menu list/status PATCH, ordering policy GET/PUT, fulfillment batch artifact references). Contract-verification CI is an external merge-time check and not contradicted by workspace evidence.

reinforcementResults.name: Authorization Boundary Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. Active policy checks are satisfied for this feature in the current workspace: UI route guards enforce employee/vendor/admin isolation and scoped-path checks (`apps/web/src/hooks.server.ts`, `apps/web/tests/hooks.test.ts`), token lifecycle coverage includes refresh success plus expiry/tamper rejection (`apps/web/tests/runtime.test.ts`), and privileged vendor APIs reject invalid role/scope claims (`src/bin/observability_runtime_service.rs` tests: `vendor_menu_api_rejects_wrong_role_token_claim`, `vendor_menu_api_rejects_mismatched_vendor_scope_claim`, `vendor_fulfillment_api_rejects_mismatched_plant_scope_claim`). Vendor MVP constraints are also represented: menu status is PATCH with `LISTED/PAUSED/DELISTED` (`src/contract.rs`, `tests/openapi_contract_platform.rs`) and export batches are traceable with immutable batch IDs/artifact references (`src/vendor_fulfillment.rs`, `tests/vendor_fulfillment_board_exports.rs`).

reinforcementResults.name: Canonical File Storage Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass based on static workspace review. Vendor Portal MVP behavior is present for current scope: vendor menu management includes dedicated status `PATCH` (`/api/v1/vendor/menu-items/{menuItemId}/status`) for `LISTED/PAUSED/DELISTED`, ordering-policy and menu filters are implemented, fulfillment board plus delivery-status transitions plus export-batch flows are implemented, and batch IDs are traceable (`fbatch-*`) with tracked artifact references. Vendor docs and operations filters are implemented in the portal UI and wired to vendor-scoped APIs only. Active file-storage policy checks are satisfied in code: upload-plan flow enforces MIME/size before persistence, fulfillment artifacts persist via the validated object-storage pipeline, no runtime DB base64 file-storage path was found, and object-storage provisioning sets managed buckets to non-anonymous access with signed URL flows.

reinforcementResults.name: Cache and Event Correctness Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: Vendor Portal MVP is implemented with required core behavior and satisfies the active policy for this feature scope. Evidence in code and tests shows: menu/status/order-window management via vendor status PATCH (`LISTED`/`PAUSED`/`DELISTED`), fulfillment board + delivery status transitions + export batches with traceable `fbatch-*` IDs and artifact references, vendor docs/object-storage and operations filtering paths, and vendor/admin route-role separation. Policy checks are covered by SQL-authoritative read paths for fulfillment plus cache write-through invalidation semantics (`src/bin/observability_runtime_service.rs`, `src/cache_backbone.rs`, `tests/cache_backbone_valkey.rs`), idempotent replay/retry handling in event consumers (`src/event_backbone.rs`, `tests/event_backbone_jetstream.rs`), and DLQ-after-retry with alert rules wired (`ops/observability/slo/alerts.yaml`).

reinforcementResults.name: UX Reliability Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: pass — no concrete blockers found for Vendor Portal MVP in the current workspace. Vendor menu/status/order-window management, fulfillment board/batch export/delivery updates, and vendor docs plus ops filters are implemented with PATCH status updates, traceable fulfillment batch IDs/artifact references, role-scoped vendor routes, loading-state duplicate-submit guards, zh-TW error copy, and desktop-first/tablet-safe layout behavior. (Test execution was not possible in this sandbox due `tsx` IPC `EPERM`, so this is based on code/contract inspection.)

reinforcementResults.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: No current-feature blockers found. The workspace implements vendor menu/status/order-window management (including dedicated status `PATCH` for `LISTED/PAUSED/DELISTED`), fulfillment board + delivery status transitions + export batch generation/retrieval with traceable `fbatch-*` IDs and artifact references, and vendor docs/operations filters in both API handlers and portal UI. Vendor routes are separated from admin-only operations, export generation is tracked/audited, desktop-first/tablet-safe presentation is configured, and optional capabilities remain behind explicit runtime feature flags. Assessment is based on code and existing tests in the repo; I did not execute tests in this read-only environment.

excludedApproaches: Exposing admin-only operations to vendor routes.
executionConstraints: Use status PATCH for 上架/暫停/下架.
Support printable exports with traceable batch IDs.
Maintain desktop-first UX with tablet-safe behavior.
